steps.title: Find the smaller map's side length
steps.content:
The problem states the smaller map is 70% shorter, meaning it retains 100% − 70% = 30% of the original side length.
$$\text{New side} = 0.30 \times 55 = 16.5 \text{ inches}$$

steps.title: Apply the inverse scaling relationship
steps.content:
Both maps represent the same geographic territory. When a map's dimensions shrink to $k$ times their original size, the miles-per-inch scale must expand by factor $1/k$ to maintain the same coverage.
With $k = 0.30$, the scale multiplier is:
$$\frac{1}{0.30} = \frac{10}{3} \approx 3.33$$

steps.title: Calculate the new scale
steps.content:
The original map has scale 1 inch = 13 miles. Apply the expansion factor:
$$\text{New scale} = 13 \times \frac{10}{3} = \frac{130}{3} \approx 43.33 \text{ miles per inch}$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.
